summary: DevMesh's stdio MCP proxy now renders tool results as compact human-readable text for Codex, while the MCP contract and daemon HTTP path keep structured JSON. title: DevMesh server startup and deployment commands
summary: DevMesh Hub Server can be run locally with `pnpm dev:server` for development, or from a build with `pnpm build` followed by `node apps/mesh-server/dist/index.js --env-file [REDACTED:sensitive-path]`. Full alpha deployment is provided by `deploy/docker-compose.yml`, started with `pnpm docker:up` or `pnpm docker:up:detached`, and the stack exposes the Hub Server on 8721, Web Admin on 5173, and the website on 3000. The server accepts configuration precedence of CLI args over process env over env file, and [REDACTED:sensitive-path] is the template for direct deployments.

title: DevMesh server startup and deployment commands
summary: DevMesh Hub Server can be run locally with `pnpm dev:server` for development, or from a build with `pnpm build` followed by `node apps/mesh-server/dist/index.js --env-file [REDACTED:sensitive-path]`. Full alpha deployment is provided by `deploy/docker-compose.yml`, started with `pnpm docker:up` or `pnpm docker:up:detached`, and the stack exposes the Hub Server on 8721, Web Admin on 5173, and the website on 3000. The server accepts configuration precedence of CLI args over process env over env file, and [REDACTED:sensitive-path] is the template for direct deployments.

title: Remaining dmx commands use readable output with JSON opt-in

title: Joining a Hub server does not require per-project reinit, but active MCP hosts may need a reload
summary: `dmx join` writes joined-server state into the global DevMesh config and identity files, so it applies across projects on the machine. Per-project DevMesh state does not need to be recreated. The daemon sync worker reads the global identity file on each run, so newly joined servers are picked up by running sync workers over time. However, an already running Codex/Claude/opencode host may need to be restarted or reloaded once to pick up any newly written MCP host configuration.

The stdio proxy reuses the shared formatter and composes its own status from the foreground runtime plus daemon inspection, avoiding parsing daemon text output.

title: dmx knowledge command manages local knowledge entries
summary: The DevMesh CLI now includes a dmx knowledge command group with get/list/update/delete subcommands. The commands use the same client runtime CRUD path as MCP tools: update appends a new JSONL version with a knowledge.updated event, and delete writes a tombstone with a knowledge.deleted event.

title: MCP tools support knowledge item CRUD
summary: DevMesh's MCP contract now exposes explicit knowledge item CRUD tools for assistants: mesh_get_knowledge, mesh_list_knowledge, mesh_update_knowledge, and mesh_delete_knowledge, alongside existing capture and search tools. Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: dmx knowledge defaults to readable text with JSON opt-in
summary: The dmx knowledge get/list/update/delete commands now default to the same concise text formatting used by MCP tool results, while preserving structured output through a per-command --json flag. The CLI imports formatMeshToolOutput from @devmesh/mcp-contracts, so knowledge command output stays aligned with assistant-facing MCP text summaries.

title: DevMesh server startup and deployment commands
summary: DevMesh Hub Server can be run locally with `pnpm dev:server` for development, or from a build with `pnpm build` followed by `node apps/mesh-server/dist/index.js --env-file [REDACTED:sensitive-path]`. Full alpha deployment is provided by `deploy/docker-compose.yml`, started with `pnpm docker:up` or `pnpm docker:up:detached`, and the stack exposes the Hub Server on 8721, Web Admin on 5173, and the website on 3000. The server accepts configuration precedence of CLI args over process env over env file, and [REDACTED:sensitive-path] is the template for direct deployments.

title: dmx knowledge command manages local knowledge entries
summary: The DevMesh CLI now includes a dmx knowledge command group with get/list/update/delete subcommands. The commands use the same client runtime CRUD path as MCP tools: update appends a new JSONL version with a knowledge.updated event, and delete writes a tombstone with a knowledge.deleted event.

title: Remaining dmx commands use readable output with JSON opt-in
summary: The dmx inbox, index rebuild, graph explore, graph edge list/add, and join commands now follow the same CLI output policy as core commands: concise readable text by default and explicit --json for scripts, tests, and integrations. Init and doctor keep their existing TUI/JSON split.

title: Joining a Hub server does not require per-project reinit, but active MCP hosts may need a reload
summary: `dmx join` writes joined-server state into the global DevMesh config and identity files, so it applies across projects on the machine. Per-project DevMesh state does not need to be recreated. The daemon sync worker reads the global identity file on each run, so newly joined servers are picked up by running sync workers over time. However, an already running Codex/Claude/opencode host may need to be restarted or reloaded once to pick up any newly written MCP host configuration.

title: Codex connection verification uses codex mcp list and DevMesh doctor/status
summary: To confirm Codex is connected to DevMesh, check Codex's MCP registry with `codex mcp list` and verify a `devmesh` entry is present, then run `dmx doctor` or `dmx status` to confirm the local DevMesh MCP launcher and sync state. The repository docs describe Codex MCP configuration via `codex mcp list` / `codex mcp add`, and DevMesh doctor/status reports MCP host and daemon health.

title: Stdio proxy should format tool output for Codex
summary: DevMesh's stdio MCP proxy now renders tool results as compact human-readable text for Codex, while the MCP contract and daemon HTTP path keep structured JSON. String tool results pass through unchanged; object results are serialized to pretty JSON for transport and then parsed or formatted at the proxy boundary.

title: dmx knowledge defaults to readable text with JSON opt-in
summary: The dmx knowledge get/list/update/delete commands now default to the same concise text formatting used by MCP tool results, while preserving structured output through a per-command --json flag. The CLI imports formatMeshToolOutput from @devmesh/mcp-contracts, so knowledge command output stays aligned with assistant-facing MCP text summaries.

title: MCP tools support knowledge item CRUD
summary: DevMesh's MCP contract now exposes explicit knowledge item CRUD tools for assistants: mesh_get_knowledge, mesh_list_knowledge, mesh_update_knowledge, and mesh_delete_knowledge, alongside existing capture and search tools. Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: dmx knowledge command manages local knowledge entries
summary: The DevMesh CLI now includes a dmx knowledge command group with get/list/update/delete subcommands. The commands use the same client runtime CRUD path as MCP tools: update appends a new JSONL version with a knowledge.updated event, and delete writes a tombstone with a knowledge.deleted event.

title: DevMesh store gitignore separates shareable knowledge from local runtime state
summary: The repository should track reviewed `.dev-mesh` project knowledge such as `knowledge/extract`, `knowledge/canonical`, `knowledge/para`, and `knowledge/edges.jsonl`, while ignoring local runtime and feedback state including `daemon.json`, `daemon.pid`, events, indexes, visualizations, queue, sync, secrets, raw knowledge, ratings, and usage logs.

title: DevMesh server startup and deployment commands
summary: DevMesh Hub Server can be run locally with `pnpm dev:server` for development, or from a build with `pnpm build` followed by `node apps/mesh-server/dist/index.js --env-file [REDACTED:sensitive-path]`. Full alpha deployment is provided by `deploy/docker-compose.yml`, started with `pnpm docker:up` or `pnpm docker:up:detached`, and the stack exposes the Hub Server on 8721, Web Admin on 5173, and the website on 3000. The server accepts configuration precedence of CLI args over process env over env file, and [REDACTED:sensitive-path] is the template for direct deployments.

title: Remaining dmx commands use readable output with JSON opt-in
summary: The dmx inbox, index rebuild, graph explore, graph edge list/add, and join commands now follow the same CLI output policy as core commands: concise readable text by default and explicit --json for scripts, tests, and integrations. Init and doctor keep their existing TUI/JSON split.

title: Joining a Hub server does not require per-project reinit, but active MCP hosts may need a reload
summary: `dmx join` writes joined-server state into the global DevMesh config and identity files, so it applies across projects on the machine. Per-project DevMesh state does not need to be recreated. The daemon sync worker reads the global identity file on each run, so newly joined servers are picked up by running sync workers over time. However, an already running Codex/Claude/opencode host may need to be restarted or reloaded once to pick up any newly written MCP host configuration.

title: Codex connection verification uses codex mcp list and DevMesh doctor/status
summary: To confirm Codex is connected to DevMesh, check Codex's MCP registry with `codex mcp list` and verify a `devmesh` entry is present, then run `dmx doctor` or `dmx status` to confirm the local DevMesh MCP launcher and sync state. The repository docs describe Codex MCP configuration via `codex mcp list` / `codex mcp add`, and DevMesh doctor/status reports MCP host and daemon health.

title: Hub Admin knowledge count does not reflect normal sync event snapshots
summary: In the current DevMesh server implementation, `/api/v1/sync/push` accepts client events into the Hub sync event log, and clients can pull/replay those snapshots locally. However, normal `knowledge.captured` / `knowledge.updated` sync snapshots are not materialized into the Hub server's core knowledge repository. Admin Overview and `/api/v1/admin/knowledge` read the server core repository, so they can still show `knowledgeItems: 0` even after daemon sync reports pushed/pulled/replayed events. Tombstones and conflicts have replay hooks, but regular knowledge snapshot replay into admin-visible knowledge is not implemented yet.

title: Hub sync now materializes knowledge snapshots into admin-visible repository
summary: DevMesh Hub Server now replays valid `payload.knowledge` snapshots from accepted sync events into the server core knowledge repository via `replayHubSyncKnowledgeSnapshots`. `/api/v1/sync/push` calls this replay before tombstone/conflict replay when events are accepted, so Admin Overview and `/api/v1/admin/knowledge` reflect synced knowledge snapshots. Invalid or missing snapshots are skipped, tombstone events are excluded, and `sync.knowledge_snapshot_replayed` audit entries record materialized items.

title: Stdio proxy should format tool output for Codex
summary: DevMesh's stdio MCP proxy now renders tool results as compact human-readable text for Codex, while the MCP contract and daemon HTTP path keep structured JSON. String tool results pass through unchanged; object results are serialized to pretty JSON for transport and then parsed or formatted at the proxy boundary.

The commands use the same client runtime CRUD path as MCP tools: update appends a new JSONL version with a knowledge.updated event, and delete writes a tombstone with a knowledge.deleted event.

title: MCP tools support knowledge item CRUD
summary: DevMesh's MCP contract now exposes explicit knowledge item CRUD tools for assistants: mesh_get_knowledge, mesh_list_knowledge, mesh_update_knowledge, and mesh_delete_knowledge, alongside existing capture and search tools. Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: MCP tool results are formatted as concise text
summary: DevMesh now centralizes MCP tool result formatting in @devmesh/mcp-contracts via formatMeshToolOutput, so stdio launcher, local HTTP proxy, and Hub MCP all return compact plain-text content instead of pretty JSON. The stdio proxy reuses the shared formatter and composes its own status from the foreground runtime plus daemon inspection, avoiding parsing daemon text output.

title: dmx knowledge command manages local knowledge entries
summary: The DevMesh CLI now includes a dmx knowledge command group with get/list/update/delete subcommands. The commands use the same client runtime CRUD path as MCP tools: update appends a new JSONL version with a knowledge.updated event, and delete writes a tombstone with a knowledge.deleted event.

title: Remaining dmx commands use readable output with JSON opt-in
summary: The dmx inbox, index rebuild, graph explore, graph edge list/add, and join commands now follow the same CLI output policy as core commands: concise readable text by default and explicit --json for scripts, tests, and integrations. Init and doctor keep their existing TUI/JSON split.

title: Joining a Hub server does not require per-project reinit, but active MCP hosts may need a reload
summary: `dmx join` writes joined-server state into the global DevMesh config and identity files, so it applies across projects on the machine. Per-project DevMesh state does not need to be recreated. The daemon sync worker reads the global identity file on each run, so newly joined servers are picked up by running sync workers over time. However, an already running Codex/Claude/opencode host may need to be restarted or reloaded once to pick up any newly written MCP host configuration.

title: Codex connection verification uses codex mcp list and DevMesh doctor/status
summary: To confirm Codex is connected to DevMesh, check Codex's MCP registry with `codex mcp list` and verify a `devmesh` entry is present, then run `dmx doctor` or `dmx status` to confirm the local DevMesh MCP launcher and sync state. The repository docs describe Codex MCP configuration via `codex mcp list` / `codex mcp add`, and DevMesh doctor/status reports MCP host and daemon health.

title: Hub Admin knowledge count does not reflect normal sync event snapshots
summary: In the current DevMesh server implementation, `/api/v1/sync/push` accepts client events into the Hub sync event log, and clients can pull/replay those snapshots locally. However, normal `knowledge.captured` / `knowledge.updated` sync snapshots are not materialized into the Hub server's core knowledge repository. Admin Overview and `/api/v1/admin/knowledge` read the server core repository, so they can still show `knowledgeItems: 0` even after daemon sync reports pushed/pulled/replayed events. Tombstones and conflicts have replay hooks, but regular knowledge snapshot replay into admin-visible knowledge is not implemented yet.

title: DevMesh store gitignore separates shareable knowledge from local runtime state
summary: The repository should track reviewed `.dev-mesh` project knowledge such as `knowledge/extract`, `knowledge/canonical`, `knowledge/para`, and `knowledge/edges.jsonl`, while ignoring local runtime and feedback state including `daemon.json`, `daemon.pid`, events, indexes, visualizations, queue, sync, secrets, raw knowledge, ratings, and usage logs.

title: MCP tools support knowledge item CRUD
summary: DevMesh's MCP contract now exposes explicit knowledge item CRUD tools for assistants: mesh_get_knowledge, mesh_list_knowledge, mesh_update_knowledge, and mesh_delete_knowledge, alongside existing capture and search tools. Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: Hub sync now materializes knowledge snapshots into admin-visible repository
summary: DevMesh Hub Server now replays valid `payload.knowledge` snapshots from accepted sync events into the server core knowledge repository via `replayHubSyncKnowledgeSnapshots`. `/api/v1/sync/push` calls this replay before tombstone/conflict replay when events are accepted, so Admin Overview and `/api/v1/admin/knowledge` reflect synced knowledge snapshots. Invalid or missing snapshots are skipped, tombstone events are excluded, and `sync.knowledge_snapshot_replayed` audit entries record materialized items.

title: Prefer hash-based anti-entropy over cursor-only sync for DevMesh knowledge
summary: DevMesh knowledge sync should treat cursors as transport optimization, not correctness proof. A more reliable design is to add canonical per-item content hashes/revision hashes plus a collection-level Merkle/state digest so clients and Hub can detect divergence and repair missing/stale/tombstoned items even when event cursors already advanced.
content: Design direction discussed: current cursor-only pushedEventIds/pullCursor flow can miss semantic divergence. Existing Hub sync already hash-chains events with `log.previousHash`/`log.hash`, but that proves event log integrity, not equality of the materialized knowledge repository. Recommended next step is a hybrid sync model: canonicalize KnowledgeItem, compute itemHash and revisionHash including parent/base hash, maintain tombstones, expose a group/project state digest or Merkle tree over sorted `{knowledgeId, revisionHash, status, updatedAt}` leaves, and use diff/repair endpoints to fetch only mismatched leaves. Keep cursors for fast incremental sync, then periodically or on reconnect run hash-based anti-entropy repair.

title: DevMesh v2 should use CRDT source of truth plus graph projections
summary: A proposed DevMesh v2 architecture uses CRDT documents as the synchronized source of truth, keeps `.dev-mesh` minimal, and models knowledge, entities, and relationships as graph primitives projected into search/Admin views.
content: Design direction: replace cursor-only knowledge synchronization with CRDT-backed sync, preferably Automerge for structured offline-first knowledge documents. `.dev-mesh` should store only config, local identity/daemon metadata, CRDT documents/changes, and rebuildable projections such as SQLite/vector/graph indexes. JSONL knowledge/events become optional export/audit projections rather than authoritative state. The domain model should separate KnowledgeItem, Entity, and Relationship/Claim nodes so semantic graph associations are first-class: knowledge mentions/supports/contradicts entities, entities alias/categorize each other, and knowledge-to-knowledge edges remain for supersedes/duplicates/contradicts. Server stores group/project CRDT state and materializes Admin/search/read models from CRDT snapshots. Cursors remain only for audit pagination or transport optimization, not correctness.

title: CRDT design keeps quality scoring as signals plus derived projections
summary: In the CRDT-based DevMesh design, dynamic knowledge scoring should store durable quality signals in CRDT and compute volatile scores in projections.

metadata.topic: dynamic knowledge scoring in CRDT design

content: Design decision: do not store `qualityScore` as authoritative CRDT state. Store durable scoring inputs such as manual confidence/weight overrides, per-member ratings, usage/adoption signals, source trust evidence, and moderation/review outcomes as CRDT fields or signal records. Projections/materializers compute `rating`, `adoptionScore`, `freshness`, and `qualityScore` from those signals during local/server materialization. Freshness is time-derived and should be recalculated, not synchronized. Per-member ratings avoid concurrent overwrite by using actor-keyed maps; usage/adoption can use append-only signal records or CRDT counters. This preserves dynamic scoring while keeping CRDT convergence stable.

title: DevMesh v2 should use a fixed CRDT-to-projection data flow and logical global CRDT sharding
summary: The CRDT design is clearer when the system is defined as a fixed flow from MCP/API writes to CRDT changes, then daemon/Hub application, then projection materialization, then queries. The server should keep a logically global CRDT namespace but shard it physically by management, knowledge, and conflict domains.

metadata.topic: core data flow and global CRDT sharding

content: Design refinement: define a single canonical data flow for DevMesh v2: MCP/API write -> CRDT change -> daemon/Hub apply -> projection materialize -> search/graph/Admin query. Treat any other intermediate state as implementation detail. On the server, keep a logically global CRDT namespace for server, groups, projects, members, knowledge, entities, relations, claims, conflicts, and signals, but allow physical sharding by domain such as server meta, group/project membership, knowledge graph data, and conflict review. Also lock terminology so CRDT, projection, entity, relation, claim, signal, conflict, and graph mean one stable thing across code and docs.

title: DevMesh knowledge must be isolated by scope to prevent cross-project contamination
summary: Project work should default to project-scoped knowledge, while global experience must be explicitly promoted and queried separately. Scope isolation should be enforced by CRDT fields, default query policy, explicit promotion flow, and ACL-filtered projections.

metadata.topic: scope isolation between project knowledge and global experience

content: Design decision: avoid mixing global experience with active project knowledge by introducing explicit scope boundaries. Recommended scopes are `private`, `project`, `group`, `org`, and `global`. Write operations default to `project` scope. Global or cross-project experience must be explicitly promoted through review/verify/promotion flow before entering the global experience pool. Query paths should default to project scope and only pull in pinned or explicitly requested global canonical knowledge. `context.build` may reference global knowledge for retrieval, but it must not implicitly write or merge global experience back into project scope. Scope must exist as a first-class field on knowledge, entities, claims, and relations, and projections must filter by scope plus ACL.

title: DevMesh should support both global and project-level connection URLs with clear precedence
summary: Connection targeting should be configurable at both device/global and project scope. Project-level connection settings must override global defaults so a single machine can use a global Hub for general experience while each project can bind to its own Hub without knowledge contamination.
content: Design decision: support two distinct connection configuration layers. Global config in `~/.dev-mesh/config.toml` stores the machine default Hub URL and default group key; project config in `<project>/.dev-mesh/config.toml` stores the project-specific Hub URL and group key. Precedence should be CLI flags > project config > global config > built-in defaults. Global connection settings are the default for unbound workflows, while project connection settings override them for that project only. This keeps the default global experience pool available without mixing knowledge into unrelated project work.

summary: Image and NewAPI video generation now reject models without a real configured pricing record before proxying upstream. Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: DevMesh v2 should use a fixed CRDT-to-projection data flow and logical global CRDT sharding
summary: The CRDT design is clearer when the system is defined as a fixed flow from MCP/API writes to CRDT changes, then daemon/Hub application, then projection materialization, then queries. The server should keep a logically global CRDT namespace but shard it physically by management, knowledge, and conflict domains.

metadata.topic: core data flow and global CRDT sharding

content: Design refinement: define a single canonical data flow for DevMesh v2: MCP/API write -> CRDT change -> daemon/Hub apply -> projection materialize -> search/graph/Admin query. Treat any other intermediate state as implementation detail. On the server, keep a logically global CRDT namespace for server, groups, projects, members, knowledge, entities, relations, claims, conflicts, and signals, but allow physical sharding by domain such as server meta, group/project membership, knowledge graph data, and conflict review. Also lock terminology so CRDT, projection, entity, relation, claim, signal, conflict, and graph mean one stable thing across code and docs.

title: DevMesh v2 采用 group-first 作为知识共享边界
summary: DevMesh v2 的产品边界已收敛为 group-first：全局只配服务器连接，项目只配 group_key，默认一个项目一个 group，多个项目显式加入同一 group 才共享知识。project 主要作为本地入口和工作单元，不再与 group 并列成两套知识模型。

title: Stdio proxy should format tool output for Codex
summary: DevMesh's stdio MCP proxy now renders tool results as compact human-readable text for Codex, while the MCP contract and daemon HTTP path keep structured JSON. String tool results pass through unchanged; object results are serialized to pretty JSON for transport and then parsed or formatted at the proxy boundary.

title: DevMesh v2 should use CRDT source of truth plus graph projections
summary: A proposed DevMesh v2 architecture uses CRDT documents as the synchronized source of truth, keeps `.dev-mesh` minimal, and models knowledge, entities, and relationships as graph primitives projected into search/Admin views.
content: Design direction: replace cursor-only knowledge synchronization with CRDT-backed sync, preferably Automerge for structured offline-first knowledge documents. `.dev-mesh` should store only config, local identity/daemon metadata, CRDT documents/changes, and rebuildable projections such as SQLite/vector/graph indexes. JSONL knowledge/events become optional export/audit projections rather than authoritative state. The domain model should separate KnowledgeItem, Entity, and Relationship/Claim nodes so semantic graph associations are first-class: knowledge mentions/supports/contradicts entities, entities alias/categorize each other, and knowledge-to-knowledge edges remain for supersedes/duplicates/contradicts. Server stores group/project CRDT state and materializes Admin/search/read models from CRDT snapshots. Cursors remain only for audit pagination or transport optimization, not correctness.

title: CRDT design keeps quality scoring as signals plus derived projections
summary: In the CRDT-based DevMesh design, dynamic knowledge scoring should store durable quality signals in CRDT and compute volatile scores in projections.

metadata.topic: dynamic knowledge scoring in CRDT design

content: Design decision: do not store `qualityScore` as authoritative CRDT state. Store durable scoring inputs such as manual confidence/weight overrides, per-member ratings, usage/adoption signals, source trust evidence, and moderation/review outcomes as CRDT fields or signal records. Projections/materializers compute `rating`, `adoptionScore`, `freshness`, and `qualityScore` from those signals during local/server materialization. Freshness is time-derived and should be recalculated, not synchronized. Per-member ratings avoid concurrent overwrite by using actor-keyed maps; usage/adoption can use append-only signal records or CRDT counters. This preserves dynamic scoring while keeping CRDT convergence stable.

title: Prefer hash-based anti-entropy over cursor-only sync for DevMesh knowledge
summary: DevMesh knowledge sync should treat cursors as transport optimization, not correctness proof. A more reliable design is to add canonical per-item content hashes/revision hashes plus a collection-level Merkle/state digest so clients and Hub can detect divergence and repair missing/stale/tombstoned items even when event cursors already advanced.
content: Design direction discussed: current cursor-only pushedEventIds/pullCursor flow can miss semantic divergence. Existing Hub sync already hash-chains events with `log.previousHash`/`log.hash`, but that proves event log integrity, not equality of the materialized knowledge repository. Recommended next step is a hybrid sync model: canonicalize KnowledgeItem, compute itemHash and revisionHash including parent/base hash, maintain tombstones, expose a group/project state digest or Merkle tree over sorted `{knowledgeId, revisionHash, status, updatedAt}` leaves, and use diff/repair endpoints to fetch only mismatched leaves. Keep cursors for fast incremental sync, then periodically or on reconnect run hash-based anti-entropy repair.

title: Release check includes npm package smoke
summary: DevMesh now has scripts/smoke-npm-package.mjs and the npm:smoke script to build, npm pack, install the CLI into a temporary global prefix, and verify installed dmx version/init/status/capture/search/graph behavior. release:check runs the smoke script in --skip-build mode after the workspace build, so publish readiness covers the actual packaged CLI entrypoint and text/JSON output contract.

Updates append a new JSONL version and deletion writes a tombstone so default searches hide removed items while audit/sync history remains available.

title: MCP tools now use explicit capability tiers
summary: DevMesh MCP tool registration now treats core tools as default-visible and registers mesh_graph_path only when the power capability is enabled. Hub server and local MCP proxy opt into power tools, while contract tests assert both the default core-only set and the power-enabled set.

title: Admin MCP must be a separate controlled entrypoint
summary: DevMesh admin HTTP already uses the same group-scoped bearer token model as the rest of the Hub APIs; there is no separate admin identity type today. Because of that, admin MCP should not be treated as a normal /mcp capability flag only. The safer design is a separate controlled admin MCP entrypoint/session that reuses Hub admin read helpers and is not exposed to ordinary clients.
